report_name: ShinyHunters Claims Amtrak Breach, Threatens to Leak 9.4 Million Records

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A prolific hacking group known for large-scale data breaches and selling stolen data. Claimed responsibility for the Amtrak breach, alleging the theft of 9.4 million records via compromised Salesforce systems and threatening to leak the data if a ransom is not paid.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through the sale or ransom of stolen data.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: The attackers likely conducted a social engineering or phishing campaign targeting Salesforce employees to steal their corporate credentials for initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Accounts
attack_patterns.description: ShinyHunters used stolen Salesforce employee credentials to access the administrative backend of their customers' environments, including Amtrak's.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: Once inside Amtrak's Salesforce environment, the attackers used built-in data export functionalities to exfiltrate 9.4 million records.

identities.name: Amtrak
identities.description: The U.S. national railroad operator, also known as the National Railroad Passenger Corporation, targeted by ShinyHunters. The group claims to have exfiltrated 9.4 million customer and corporate records.

identities.name: Salesforce
identities.description: A third-party service provider for Amtrak. ShinyHunters allegedly compromised Amtrak's Salesforce environment by targeting Salesforce employees with social engineering to gain initial access.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Dark Web Forum
infrastructure.description: A dark web forum used by the ShinyHunters group to post their claim of the Amtrak data breach and threaten to leak the stolen data.

indicators.name: Anomalous Large Salesforce Data Export
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Large Salesforce Data Export
id: 2f8b1c9a-7d3e-4b0f-8c1d-9e5a6b3c2d1f
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects a large data export operation within Salesforce, which could indicate data exfiltration by a compromised account, as seen in the ShinyHunters attack against Amtrak.
references:
  - https://www.scmagazine.com/brief/amtrak-allegedly-breached-by-shinyhunters-massive-data-leak-threatened
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-16
tags:
  - attack.t1530
  - attack.collection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate large-scale data backups or migrations.
level: medium
logsource:
  product: salesforce
  service: api
detection:
  selection:
    event.action: 'export'
    event.category: 'data'
    # This threshold is an example and should be tuned for the specific environment
    data.exported_rows: '>100000'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects anomalous large data exports from Salesforce, which was the method ShinyHunters allegedly used to exfiltrate 9.4 million records from Amtrak.
